## More than 20 years of Excellence in Immigration Law

## What Is the EB-2 NIW?
The EB-2 National Interest Waiver (NIW) is a green card option for certain foreign nationals who have exceptional ability or hold advanced degrees. What makes this visa especially appealing is that it does not require an employer sponsor or labor certification, a process that is often time-consuming and complex. Instead, eligible petitioners can self-petition for a green card if their work is considered to be in the national interest of the United States.
- No Work Restrictions
- Allow self-petitioning
- A path to U.S. citizenship

## EB-2 NIW Visa Requirements: Do you Qualify?
To qualify for the EB-2 NIW, petitioners must have an advanced degrees or exceptional ability, and show their work provides significant value to the United States. Eligibility is determined through a three-prong test set by Matter of Dhanasar.
- Substantial Merit & National Importance
- Well-positioned to Advance the Endeavor
- Labor Certification Waiver Benefits the U.S.
- Advanced Degree or Exceptional Ability
If you don’t meet the education requirement, you may qualify based on exceptional ability. This means you have a level of expertise that is much higher than others in your field, whether in science, art, or business.

## Frequently Asked Questions about EB-2 NIW
The standard EB-2 visa process requires a Labor Certification, which can take 15 months to 3 years. This involves proving no qualified U.S. workers are available for the job. Alternatively, the National Interest Waiver (NIW) allows individuals to bypass this requirement if it’s in the US’s best interest.
With a NIW, individuals can self-petition, waiving job offer and Labor Certification requirements. While NIW approvals can be more challenging, working with an experienced attorney can improve the chances of a favorable outcome.
The USCIS Administrative Appeals Office ruling in Matter of Dhanasar (2016) established a three-prong test for EB-2 Nation al In ter est Waiver (NIW) eligibility. Petitioners must demonstrate that : (1) the proposed endeavor has substantial merit and national importance, (2) they are well-positioned to advance the endeavor, and (3) waiving job offer and labor certification requirements would benefit the U.S. The ruling also outlines factors USCIS considers for ea ch pr ong. Petitioners must also meet EB-2 visa requirements, in that they hold an advanced degree or demonstrate exceptional ability.
Pursuing NIW offers a significant advantage over employer-sponsored permanent residence: control . NIW allows for self-petitioning , meaning that you are not reliant on your employer, which provides independence and stability. If your employer experiences financial difficulties or terminates your employment, your NIW application remains unaffected. This autonomy becomes increasingly important as the application process can be lengthy.
The EB-2 NIW process is complex and highly detailed. Success depends not only on meeting eligibility criteria but also on presenting a well-documented, persuasive petition that demonstrates your qualifications.
